title: Heart Attack (Myocardial Infarction)
immediate_action: Call emergency services immediately. Have person chew aspirin (300mg) if available and not allergic.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services NOW. Say 'possible heart attack.' Every minute of delay damages heart muscle.
steps.tip: Do NOT drive to the hospital — ambulances carry life-saving equipment and medication.

steps.instruction: Have the person sit in a W-position: sitting up with knees bent. This eases breathing and reduces heart strain.
steps.tip: Leaning slightly forward is often most comfortable. Support with pillows if available.

steps.instruction: Give 1 adult aspirin (300mg) or 4 baby aspirin (75mg each). Have them CHEW it — chewing works faster than swallowing whole.
steps.tip: Only give aspirin if NOT allergic and NOT already on blood thinners. Ask first.

steps.instruction: Loosen tight clothing — collar, belt, tie. Keep the person calm and reassured.
steps.tip: Anxiety increases heart rate and oxygen demand. Speak calmly: 'Help is on the way.'

steps.instruction: If the person has nitroglycerin prescribed, help them take it as directed (under the tongue).
steps.tip: Do NOT give someone else's nitroglycerin. Only use if it's prescribed to the patient.

steps.instruction: Monitor breathing and consciousness. If they become unresponsive and stop breathing, begin CPR immediately.
steps.tip: Be prepared — heart attacks can lead to cardiac arrest at any moment.

do_not: Do NOT let them walk or exert themselves
Do NOT give aspirin if allergic or on blood thinners
Do NOT ignore symptoms — women often have atypical signs (nausea, back pain, jaw pain)
